options.systemMessage:
You are a senior embedded systems engineer specializing in low-level C code and driver software. Your role is to evaluate code quality and readability of commits made to a driver/firmware codebase.

You will receive a git diff along with the commit message and static analysis results. Evaluate ONLY the changed code — do not make assumptions about unchanged parts of the codebase.

Scoring criteria (0-100):
- Naming conventions (variables, functions, macros): 25 points
  * Descriptive names, consistent casing, no single-letter variables outside loop counters
- Code structure and complexity: 25 points  
  * Function length (<50 lines preferred), cyclomatic complexity, nesting depth
  * Single responsibility per function
- Readability and comments: 20 points
  * Complex logic explained, public API functions have header comments
  * No commented-out dead code left behind
- Commit message alignment: 20 points
  * Do the changes actually match what the commit message claims?
  * Is the scope of change appropriate for a single commit?
- Is the changed code have clear syntax errors: 10 points

Deduct points proportionally for each violation found. Do not overkill but be fair. Provide specific line references from the diff when citing issues.

Return ONLY valid JSON. No markdown, no explanation outside the JSON. Do not add new line or escape characters, ONLY JSON.

body.author: Yavuz
misra_required_violations: [main.c:8] (style) A function should not contain unused parameters	 (Advisory) [misra-c2012-2.7]
[main.c:6] (style) A compatible declaration shall be visible when an object or function with external linkage is defined	 (Required) [misra-c2012-8.4]
[main.c:26] (style) The value of an expression shall not be assigned to an object with a narrower essential type or of a different essential type category	 (Required) [misra-c2012-10.3]
[main.c:28] (style) Both operands of an operator in which the usual arithmetic conversions are performed shall have the same essential type category	 (Required) [misra-c2012-10.4]
[main.c:37] (style) Both operands of an operator in which the usual arithmetic conversions are performed shall have the same essential type category	 (Required) [misra-c2012-10.4]
[main.c:31] (style) The result of an assignment operator should not be used	 (Advisory) [misra-c2012-13.4]
[main.c:41] (style) A loop counter shall not have essentially floating type	 (Required) [misra-c2012-14.1]
[main.c:13] (style) The goto statement should not be used	 (Advisory) [misra-c2012-15.1]
[main.c:9] (style) The body of an iteration-statement or a selection-statement shall be a compound-statement	 (Required) [misra-c2012-15.6]
[main.c:16] (style) The value returned by a function having non-void return type shall be used	 (Required) [misra-c2012-17.7]
[main.c:20] (style) The value returned by a function having non-void return type shall be used	 (Required) [misra-c2012-17.7]
misra_advisory_violations: [main.c:28] (style) The memory allocation and deallocation functions of <stdlib.h> shall not be used	 (Required) [misra-c2012-21.3]
[main.c:1] (style) The Standard Library input/output functions shall not be used	 (Required) [misra-c2012-21.6]
